query: Diff the reference and student solutions for this paired-cholesterol problem and give formative feedback. Problem: 24 patients, before/after cholesterol, α = 0.05, Shapiro on differences gives p = 0.018. Reference solution: identifies paired design → Shapiro on differences rejects Normality → runs Wilcoxon signed-rank → reports the result with effect size. Student attempt: treats the data as independent two-sample → runs Shapiro on each column separately (both pass) → runs a pooled two-sample t-test → reports the t-statistic and p-value. Audience tier: undergrad-intro. Grading mode: formative.
expected_behavior: Classifies step 1 (design) as category (B) wrong-answer-right-reasoning-with-one-identifiable-misstep, where the student treated paired data as independent groups.

Marks the downstream steps (assumption check, test choice, report) as cascade from step 1 rather than as fresh independent errors, recognizing that the rest of the work correctly follows from the wrong starting decision.

Writes the feedback in student-facing voice that names the principle (e.g., 'design determines the test family before any numbers are computed' or 'paired data = same subjects measured twice'), not just the symptom ('you used the wrong test').
